html:
European Commission, Daily News, Brussels, June 24, 2026.

The Commission plans to issue EU bonds worth up to €80 billion during the second half of 2026. The European Commission has announced its intention to issue up to €80 billion in EU bonds in...
tldr: The EU plans to borrow up to 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026 by issuing new bonds, known as EU bonds.
lattlast:
The European Commission, which is the government of the EU, has announced its intention to borrow money during the second half of 2026. They plan to do this by issuing so-called EU bonds with a value of up to 80 billion euros.

A bond is a certificate of a loan. When the EU issues bonds, it borrows money from investors and promises to repay the money later. This is done to allow the EU to finance important projects and activities within the union.

The plan involves managing these large sums of money during the last few months of 2026. This is part of the EU's economic plan to ensure that funds are available for future needs.
